refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation link availability at or above the customer-defined target throughout the operational period.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR to explicitly state the performance metric (link availability) and the operational context ensures clarity and testability.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure link uptime statistics over the agreed evaluation period and compare against the customer-defined availability target.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor teleoperation link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and recover from link failures promptly.
FR3: Report link availability status to relevant stakeholders.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement redundant network links with automatic failover.
DP2: Use link health monitoring and predictive diagnostics.
DP3: Integrate link status reporting and alerting mechanisms.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement redundant network links with automatic failover.

coupling_check.notes: Redundant network links logically isolate link availability concerns, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Risk of increased system complexity impacting maintainability.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding DP2 and DP3 in subsequent refinement levels to enhance monitoring and reporting.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical and embedded systems responsible for network link hardware and failover logic.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Handles redundant network connections and failover signaling.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Sends link status and availability data to NGHE Solution and Safety Services.

module_containment_scores.notes: High-level coordination but less direct control over physical link redundancy.
Primary containment of network link hardware and failover logic; minimal diffusion.
Receives link status but does not control redundancy mechanisms.
Monitors safety alerts but not involved in link availability control.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Placing the DP in the Vehicle module centralizes control of physical network redundancy, minimizing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential complexity in Vehicle module may increase integration effort.
Dependency on Vehicle hardware capabilities for redundancy.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Evaluate integration complexity with Teleoperation and NGHE Solution modules in next design iteration.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (redundant network links with failover) directly addresses the refined FR of achieving required link availability.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion as redundancy logic is contained within Vehicle module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support Vehicle as the best ownership candidate.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification via telemetry data and link uptime logs is feasible.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility to Vehicle module for physical link availability.

recommended_revisions: Consider adding monitoring and reporting DPs in future refinement levels.
Assess integration interfaces with Teleoperation and Safety Services for alerting.

decision.justification: While the current decomposition is sound, adding monitoring and reporting sub-FRs and corresponding DPs will improve completeness and traceability.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor teleoperation link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and recover from link failures promptly.
FR3: Report link availability status to relevant stakeholders.

revise.why: To decompose the high-level FR into atomic sub-FRs that cover monitoring, recovery, and reporting aspects.

assumptions: Vehicle module has capability to implement failover logic.
open_risks: Integration complexity with Teleoperation and Safety Services modules.
Potential hardware limitations in Vehicle module.
evidence_needed: Verification test results of failover mechanisms.
